I want it to: standardize the fields (phone, e-mail, name), find and merge the duplicates, classify each record (for example: hot/cold lead, by segment, by region) and flag the ones that are incomplete or suspicious. The cases it isn't sure about, I want it to set aside for me to review in a queue, instead of guessing.

The most important part: if the processing stops midway — from an error, from volume, for any reason — it has to resume from where it left off, without reprocessing what it already did or duplicating anything. I want to track the progress (how many are done, how many are left, how many need my review) and, in the end, have the clean, classified base ready to use, with a summary of what was done.

Once it's running, I'll want to schedule it to run on its own every time a new batch lands in a folder, without me kicking it off by hand.

dentro: Import of a large batch; cleansing (standardization + dedup) + classification by rule/agent
**Durable processing with automatic resume from where it left off (no reprocessing/duplicating)**
Review queue for the doubtful cases (human approval); progress dashboard
Final clean/classified base + summary of what was done
fora: Automatic ingestion from a live external source (queue/API)
Orchestrating multiple concurrent batches with priority
evol: Trigger on arrival from an external source via system connectors (v2)

dims.t: Batch linked to the records and to each one's state (raw/cleansed/classified/in review); dedup and standardization rules; classification standardized status
Import screen; batch progress dashboard (processed/pending/in review); review queue for the doubtful ones with correction
**Durable large-batch processing that automatically resumes after a failure without reprocessing or duplicating; human approval gate on the review queue**
Standardizes, finds duplicates by similarity, classifies each record and sets the doubtful ones aside instead of guessing
None external in v1 (operates on the imported file); (evolution) trigger from an external source
PII in the records (contacts/registrations) masked before the LLM; trail of what changed in each record; original base preserved

UC-021.kpi: time to get a batch ready for use (from hours/days in Excel to automatic processing) + error/duplicate rate in the final base (lower than manual handling). In the end, you'll say: "I throw in the messy base and get it back clean and classified, without someone spending the day in Excel and without starting over when it stalls".

UC-022.dor_hook: I send a proposal and it dies in silence — not because the customer said no, but because nobody sent the second touch.
UC-022.problema: I send out proposals and quotes all the time, and most of them die in silence — not because the customer said no, but because nobody sent the second, the third touch. Follow-up depends on me remembering, and I don't: it disappears in the rush. Something I asked the customer for (a document, a sign-off) sits in limbo. I know I'm leaving money on the table out of pure lack of persistence. I'd want a relentless tracker: one that nudges every stalled proposal, at the right time, in the right tone, until there's a reply or I tell it to stop — but that shows me before speaking in my name.
UC-022.pedido:
I'm always sending proposals and quotes to my customers, and a big chunk simply goes cold because I can't keep the momentum going. The customer didn't reply? I should send a second touch in a few days, then a third, but in the rush that doesn't happen and the opportunity dies. Same goes for pending items: I asked for a document or a sign-off and it just sits there with nobody chasing it.

I want a tracker that won't give up. For each proposal sent, open quote, or pending item, it should track the time since the last contact and, once it passes the point, put together the next touch — a light reminder first, then something more direct — and keep pushing at intervals over the days/weeks until the customer replies or I tell it to stop. Each proposal has to carry the history of how many touches have gone out and what the customer replied.

Rule: every message that goes out in my name I want to review and approve first — at least the first ones. When the customer replies with something that needs a decision (wants a discount, wants to renegotiate, has an objection), the tracker stops and hands it to me with the history. I want to see a dashboard of what's open and how long each thing has been stalled.

Once it's running, I'll want it to tune the touch intervals on its own based on the customer's behavior (whoever tends to reply fast gets nudged sooner).

dentro: Durable tracking of a proposal/quote/pending item (time since the last touch)
Relentless multi-touch cadence with long timers + stop on reply/order
Composing the touch by stage; preview + approval
Escalation when the reply needs a decision; "stalled for X" dashboard

dims.t: Trackable item (proposal/quote/pending item) linked to the Customer; touch count and reply history; stage standardized status
Dashboard of items stalled by time; a record with the timeline of touches; preview of the next touch
Durable sequence over weeks with per-item timers; recovery; automatic stop on a reply or the owner's order
Agent decides when and how to nudge, drafts the touch in the tone of the stage and escalates when the reply calls for a decision
WhatsApp (connected channel) for the touches; e-mail as an alternative
Preview + approval before sending; masked PII; trail of every touch

UC-022.kpi: reply/conversion rate on proposals that would have gone cold — revenue recovered through persistence. In the end, you'll say: "proposals I'd have forgotten started moving on their own again; I stopped leaving money on the table". Measurable by: % of stalled proposals that start getting replies again after the cadence.

objecoes.q: Will it flood my customer with messages and turn into spam?
objecoes.a: No. It sends the touches at intervals and stops the moment the customer replies or you tell it to stop; and you approve the messages before they go out.

objecoes.q: Does it decide on its own when the customer asks for a discount?
objecoes.a: No. When the reply needs a decision (discount, renegotiation, objection), it stops and hands it to you with the history.

objecoes.q: Does it put the proposal together for me?
objecoes.a: Not in this version; it handles the persistence in the follow-up. The proposal editor is an evolution.

UC-023.dor_hook: The renewal catches me off guard and, in the rush not to lose the customer, I end up giving away too much of a discount.
UC-023.problema: Contracts and subscriptions that expire catch me off guard. I find out it was time to renew when the customer has already left or when I'm scrambling on the last day — and in the rush, I give away too much of a discount just to not lose them. I don't have a cadence that starts the renewal conversation ahead of time, and I don't have control over who can give how much of a discount. I'd want the system to start the renewal on its own, at the right time, and any discount above what I allow to go through me before it's offered.
UC-023.pedido:
I work with recurring contracts and subscriptions, and the renewal is always a last-minute scramble. By the time I notice, it's already expiring, and then to not lose the customer I end up giving a rushed discount, with no criteria. I want to flip this around: start the renewal ahead of time and with control.

I need the system to know each customer's renewal date and start a renewal cadence ahead of time (about 45 and 15 days before, for example): a touch letting them know the time is coming, then the renewal proposal. I want it to track whether the customer replied and keep nudging tactfully until it closes or I take over.

The part I won't budge on is the discount. Each customer/plan has a maximum discount that can be offered without talking to me. If the renewal needs a discount above that, the system can't offer it on its own — it has to stop and ask me for approval, showing the customer's history and the reason why. I want it logged who approved each off-standard discount.

Once it's running, I'll want a quarterly renewals dashboard: what renewed, what's at risk and how much revenue that represents.

dentro: Renewal date per customer + early cadence (e.g., D-45, D-15)
Renewal proposal with durable tracking
**Discount authority:** above the threshold → human approval with history
Trail of who approved each off-standard discount

dims.t: Customer/Contract with a renewal date, plan and maximum allowed discount; renewal history
List of renewals by window (due soon, at risk); a record with history; discount-approval screen
Durable early cadence with timers; chained authority (discount above the threshold → approver)
Detects what's renewing, proposes the timing and the message; builds the case when it needs discount approval
Existing channel (WhatsApp/e-mail through the connected channel) for the renewal cadence
Discount authority as a business object (proof + undo); trail of the approvals

UC-023.kpi: rate of renewals handled before expiry + average discount under control (within authority). In the end, you'll say: "I renew at the right time and stop giving rushed discounts; I know who approved each exception". Measurable by: % of renewals started before expiry and % of discounts within authority.

objecoes.q: Does the system offer a discount to my customer on its own?
objecoes.a: Only up to the limit you set. A discount above that it won't offer — it stops and asks you for approval, showing the customer's history.

objecoes.q: Does it start the renewal without me telling it to?
objecoes.a: It kicks off the cadence ahead of time (for example, 45 and 15 days before), but the messages go through your OK and you can take over at any moment.

objecoes.q: Do I need a separate system to bill the renewal?
objecoes.a: The billing itself is out of scope in this version; the focus is renewing at the right time and with the discount under control.

UC-024.dor_hook: Every month I match the bank statement against my system by hand, line by line — it takes hours, and one slip is money.
UC-024.problema: Every month I (or my accountant) sit down to match what the bank shows against what my system says — and it's a manual nightmare: statement on one side, my accounts on the other, checking line by line to find what doesn't match (a fee I didn't record, a payment that didn't land, a mismatched amount). It takes hours, it's easy to slip, and a slip here is money. The worst part is that, when something doesn't match, I'm not sure whether I recorded the entry wrong or the amount came in wrong — and I can't "trust a hunch" from a system, because it's financial reconciliation: it has to be right. I'd want the system to pull both sides, match what's identical on its own, show me only the discrepancies to resolve, and let me check where each number came from.
UC-024.pedido:
Every month I need to reconcile my bank statement against the entries in my financial system, and today that's done by hand, line by line, which takes hours and is full of errors. To start, I'll upload two files: the bank statement (date, description, amount, type) and my payables/receivables list (expected date, description, amount, status).

I want the system to match the two sides on its own: pair each entry with the corresponding line in the statement by amount, date and description, and mark what reconciles. What doesn't match, I want it to separate into clear discrepancies: "expected payment that didn't land", "debit on the statement with no entry", "amount different from expected" — for me to work each one. It's money, so this can't be a guess from the system: the check has to be exact, and I want to be able to see, for each reconciliation, exactly which two lines were paired and why (the verification read-back). If it proposes a match that isn't obvious, I want to approve it before it's marked as reconciled.

In the end, I want a close: total reconciled, total open, list of discrepancies, all logged for me (or my accountant) to audit later.

Once it's running, I want it to pull the statement straight from the bank and the entries straight from my system, without me uploading files — to reconcile continuously, not just at month-end.

dentro: Reconciliation of two sides **imported by file** (statement × entries) with matching by amount/date/description
**Deterministic** verification of the match + verification read-back (see which lines were paired)
Queue of typed discrepancies with human approval on the non-obvious matches
Auditable close (reconciled × open)
fora: **Direct reading from the bank/ERP (the real pain)**
evol: **Statement/system of record directly via system connectors — v2 (the )**
Write-off/bookkeeping in the ERP via connector (v2)
Continuous reconciliation (not just monthly) via connector (v2)

dims.t: Entries and statement lines linked by a reconciliation, with an integrity rule (one line matches one; the total balances) and an auditable ledger; standardized list of status and of discrepancy type
Reconciliation dashboard (reconciled/open/discrepant); queue of typed discrepancies; verification read-back of each match (the two lines side by side)
Reconciliation workflow (import → match → work discrepancies → close), durable, with an human approval gate on the non-obvious matches
Proposes the matches by amount/date/description and types the discrepancies — but the **verdict is deterministic** (the agent proposes, the verification confirms; it doesn't "think it matches")
**system connectors (OAuth) to pull the statement from the bank and the entries from the ERP; it's the real source of the value (v1 operates by file)**
Financial PII (accounts, amounts) masked; tamper-proof trail of every reconciliation and adjustment (ledger); verification read-back as proof; role-based permissions

UC-024.kpi: reconciliation close time (from manual hours to automatic minutes) + discrepancies caught with precision (money that wouldn't have matched and would have slipped through). In the end, you'll say: "I reconcile in minutes, sure it's right and able to prove each match — it's not a hunch".

UC-025.dor_hook: A customer doesn't tell you they're leaving — they just go quiet, and I only notice once they're gone.
UC-025.problema: A customer doesn't tell you they're leaving — they just go quiet. They stop buying, stop replying, fade out little by little, and by the time I notice they've gone fully cold or they're already gone. I can't keep an eye on every single account all the time to catch who's slipping away; I only react once someone has already canceled. I'd want someone watching the base for me — noticing "it's been a while since so-and-so bought/replied/showed up" and already starting to pull that person back with a reactivation playbook, alerting me and asking for my OK before sending. That way I act while there's still time, instead of discovering the loss at month-end.
UC-025.pedido:
I have a base of about 200 recurring customers. My problem is that I lose customers without noticing: they buy less, fade out little by little, and I only find out once they're gone. I can't watch each one all the time. I'll start by loading a spreadsheet with my customers (name, contact, last purchase, average value, usual frequency).

I want a system that watches this base on its own and alerts me when someone is cooling off — for example, a customer who usually buys every month and hasn't shown up in 45 days, or who stopped replying. When that happens, I want it to already start a reactivation script for that person: a check-in message on WhatsApp, a come-back offer, a "we've missed you" — in the right tone for their profile. But nothing goes to the customer without my OK: the system prepares it and shows me, I approve before it goes out.

On my screen I want a dashboard of the base split by health (active / cooling / dormant) and, for each at-risk customer, why they dropped to that band and what's already been tried to bring them back. And I want it logged what was done with each one.

Once it's running, I'll want it to recognize when the reactivation worked (the customer bought again) and close the case on its own, and to show me how much revenue I recovered that way.

dentro: Customers + activity signals (last purchase, frequency, reply) imported
Cooling detection by inactivity rule; health band per customer
Reactivation playbook fired on the trigger, with an human approval gate before each contact
Base dashboard by health + history; WhatsApp as the channel
fora: Automatic reading of purchases from an external system (ERP/e-commerce)
evol: Bridge to UC-007 (collections) when the cooling is a late payment
Recognize reactivation and measure recovered revenue (v2)

dims.t: Customer linked to activity signals (last purchase, frequency, reply) with history; spreadsheet import; health-band standardized status
Base dashboard by band (active/cooling/dormant); per-customer cockpit with the reason for the band and what's already been tried
**Continuous monitoring that fires the reactivation playbook on detecting inactivity, advancing through contacts over time with an human approval gate**
**Detects the cooling pattern (deviation from the usual frequency), decides the reactivation script and the tone by profile, prioritizes who to reach**
WhatsApp (connected channel) for the reactivation; e-mail as an alternative
Customers' PII masked; nothing is sent without an OK; trail of every reactivation attempt per customer

UC-025.kpi: customers reactivated who were cooling off (bought again) + recurring revenue preserved that would have been lost to a silent fade-out. In the end, you'll say: "I no longer lose customers without noticing — the system alerts me when someone is slipping away and already starts bringing them back".

UC-026.dor_hook: My month-end close lives in a spreadsheet and in people's heads — and one forgotten step turns into a penalty.
UC-026.problema: Every month I have a pile of routines that can't fail: close the books, reconcile, pay the obligations, issue reports, send things to the accountant — each with its deadline, its order and its owner. Today that lives in a checklist spreadsheet and in people's heads; when someone is out, forgets a step or does it out of order, it means a penalty, a delay or rework. I don't have a view of "where are we in this month's close" or proof that each obligation was met on time. I'd want these recurring routines to run as an orchestrated process — each task on time, in the right order, with the owner notified, and showing me what's pending and what's done, month after month, without depending on someone remembering.
UC-026.pedido:
I have a monthly admin routine that today lives in a spreadsheet and in people's heads, and that costs me penalties and rework when something is forgotten or done out of order. I'll start by registering the recurring tasks of my month-end close (for example: check payables by the 2nd; reconcile the bank by the 3rd; pay taxes and dues by their due dates; close billing by the 5th; send the documents to the accountant by the 7th), each with its deadline, its owner and what depends on what.

I want this to run as an orchestrated process every month, on its own: at the start of the cycle the system opens the tasks, notifies each owner, chases whoever is falling behind, and respects the order (it doesn't let "close billing" run before "reconcile", for example). The tasks that need my sign-off before completing (paying above a threshold, sending the close) go through me. I want to see a dashboard of the current cycle: what's pending, what's late, what's done — and the history of previous months, to prove each obligation was met on time.

Once it's running, I'll want it to alert me ahead of time about next month's obligations and to let me use the same routine template for more than one company I manage.

UC-027.dor_hook: My customers ask for everything over WhatsApp — booking, rescheduling, confirming — and one person answers that all day long.
UC-027.problema: My customers talk to me over WhatsApp for everything: booking a time, rescheduling, confirming attendance, asking about the status of an order. One person on the team answers that all day long, and even so, outside business hours the customer is left without a reply — by morning the queue has piled up. Reschedules get lost in the middle of conversations and no-shows nobody prevents. Each customer's history is scattered across threads. I'd want WhatsApp itself to resolve the request — book, reschedule, confirm, give status — without the customer having to download or log into anything, and only the delicate case reaching a person.
UC-027.pedido:
My customers reach out over WhatsApp all the time to handle day-to-day things: book a time, reschedule, confirm they'll show up, or ask how their order/service is doing. Today one person on my team answers all of that by hand, which eats up the day and still leaves people without a reply outside business hours.

I want the WhatsApp service to resolve these requests on its own, in the conversation, without the customer having to install an app or create a login. When someone writes "I'd like to book for Thursday", the service should understand, check availability, offer times and confirm — and the same for rescheduling and canceling. Before appointments, I want it to send the attendance confirmation on its own and update the calendar based on the reply. If the customer asks about the status of something, it answers with the right information.

On my side (internal), I want to see the calendar and the records organized — each customer with their history, personal data (like ID numbers) protected, and a log of what was done in each conversation. When the customer complains, has an urgent matter or asks for something out of the ordinary, the service doesn't make things up: it hands the conversation to a person on my team with the full history, so the customer doesn't have to repeat themselves.

Once it's running, I'll want to add a reminder the day before and a waitlist that fills slots that open up from cancellations.

dentro: Agent resolves booking/rescheduling/confirming/status in the conversation (no login)
Internal records + calendar; ID number detected and protected

In the end, you'll say: "WhatsApp became a booking that resolves itself; my team only steps in for the delicate case". Measurable by: self-resolution rate and attendance rate after confirmation.

objecoes.q: Does my customer need to download an app or create a login?
objecoes.a: No. They resolve everything in the usual WhatsApp conversation — book, reschedule, confirm — without installing anything.

objecoes.q: Does the service confirm something it shouldn't?
objecoes.a: No. It only resolves what's within the rules; a complaint, an urgency or an out-of-the-ordinary request it hands to a person on the team with the history.

objecoes.q: Is my customers' data kept protected?
objecoes.a: Yes. Personal data like ID numbers is protected, and each company only sees its own customers.

UC-028.dor_hook: My customers book by phone and WhatsApp all day, and outside business hours nobody can book at all.
UC-028.problema: My customers book appointments with me by phone and WhatsApp, and it eats up my front desk's day: people calling to book, reschedule, ask whether there's an opening on Thursday. Outside business hours, nobody books — the customer gives up or goes to the competitor who lets them book online. And since the calendar is run by hand, sometimes I double-book the same slot or forget to block off a break. I'd want a page I could share (on Instagram, in the link in bio, on Google) where the customer sees my free times and books on their own, at any hour, without calling me and without downloading anything — and that it lands straight in my calendar, already blocking the slot, with the confirmation going to the customer automatically.
UC-028.pedido:
I run an appointment-based business (about 3 professionals seeing customers), and today every booking goes through phone or WhatsApp, which eats up my front desk and makes me lose customers outside business hours. I'll start by registering my services (name, duration, professional) and each professional's availability (the days and hours they work).

I want a public page I can share with a link (paste it on Instagram, on Google, on my site), where the customer picks the service, sees the times that are actually free and books on their own — without calling me, without creating an account, without downloading an app, at any hour of the day or night. When they book, the slot has to land straight in my calendar and get blocked right away (so two customers don't grab the same slot), and the confirmation goes to them automatically with the details.

On my side, I want to see the calendar organized by professional, with the bookings that came from the page, and be able to block off times (lunch, days off) so they don't show as free. The data the customer provides (name, phone) is kept carefully, since it's a page open on the internet.

Once it's running, I'll want it to send a reminder and ask for attendance confirmation the day before, and to offer rescheduling through the page itself if the customer needs it.

In the end, you'll say: "my customer books on their own, at any hour, and it lands straight in the calendar — my front desk stopped living on the phone".

UC-029.dor_hook: Quote requests come in piecemeal over WhatsApp, e-mail and Instagram, and there's always a missing detail.
UC-029.problema: Quote requests reach me from all sides: one comes over WhatsApp, another in an Instagram DM, another by e-mail, and there's almost always something missing — the customer doesn't give the square footage, doesn't send the photo, doesn't mention the deadline, and then the back-and-forth starts just to gather the basics. By the end of the day I have requests scattered across three places, none of them organized, and every so often one falls through the cracks and I lose the sale. I'd want a page I could share with a link, where the customer fills in what I need to know (with the right, required fields), attaches whatever's needed, and it lands in one organized place, ready for me to respond — without me having to hunt down information.
UC-029.pedido:
I run a custom-service business (about 40-50 quote requests a month), and today these requests come in loose over WhatsApp, e-mail and Instagram, almost always incomplete, which forces me to keep asking for the basics before I can even quote. I'll start by defining the data every request needs to have: the customer's name and contact, the type of service, a description, the desired deadline, and the option to attach photos or a reference file.

I want a public page I can share with a link (Instagram, WhatsApp, my site), where the customer fills in that form on their own, without calling and without creating an account, with the right fields and the required ones marked as required, so no half-baked request comes in. When they submit, the request has to land in an organized inbox on my side, with all the data together, and the customer gets an automatic confirmation that the request arrived.

On my side, I want to see the list of requests that came in, with each one's status (new, under review, answered), and be able to mark where each request stands. Since it's a page open on the internet collecting people's contact info, the data the customer provides has to be kept carefully.

Once it's running, I'll want requests of the same type to come in pre-classified, and to be able to put together a standard quote reply from what the customer filled in.

dentro: **Public page (no login) with a form** with configurable, required fields and attachments

UC-030.dor_hook: Customers keep calling to ask where their order is, and my team repeats the same answer all day long.
UC-030.problema: My customer is always asking me where their order is — they call, they WhatsApp, and my team spends the day repeating the same answer: "it's in production", "it shipped yesterday", "there's one step left". Each inquiry interrupts the work, and when it takes a while to reply the customer gets anxious and complains. The information exists on my side; the problem is that it doesn't reach them without someone stopping to type it out. I'd want a page where the customer themselves, with a link or a code, could see which stage their order is at, genuinely up to date, without having to call me — and that when the stage changes they'd already be notified.
UC-030.pedido:
I run a business that fulfills orders that take a few days across stages (about 30-40 orders in progress at any moment), and today my customer keeps calling or WhatsApping all the time to find out where theirs stands, and my team loses the day repeating the same answer. I'll start by registering my orders with the customer, an order identifier and the stages it goes through (for example: received, in production, ready, delivered).

I want a public page where the customer tracks their order by a unique link or code, without having to call, without creating an account and without downloading anything, seeing only the current stage and the stage history of THEIR order — never another customer's data. When I (or my team) update the stage on my side, the page has to reflect it right away, and the customer gets an automatic notice that the order moved forward.

On my side, I want a simple screen to move each order between stages and see the overall list of in-progress orders by stage. Since the link is open, each customer can only see their own order.

Once it's running, I'll want the customer to be able to leave a comment or attach something on their order's page, and to be able to estimate the completion date of each stage.

Simple internal screen to move orders between stages; list by stage
Isolation by order: each link only sees its own

UC-031.dor_hook: My customer asks me for the same duplicate copy and the same document over and over, and I'm forever resending it by hand.
UC-031.problema: My customer is forever asking me for the same things: a duplicate of the invoice, the signed contract, the receipt for the last purchase, the history of what they've done with me. And every time it's me (or my team) stopping to find the document and resend it by hand, by e-mail or WhatsApp. It's repetitive, it slows things down, and when a document goes missing it becomes a problem. I'd want an area where the customer themselves could log in and grab what's theirs on their own — the documents, the history, the duplicate copy — without depending on me, and with the guarantee that each one only sees their own data, never another customer's.
UC-031.pedido:
I provide a recurring service to about 200 customers and I'm forever resending the same things by hand: duplicate copies, contracts, receipts, history. I'll start by organizing, per customer, their documents (contracts, receipts, invoices) and the relationship history (what they've already hired, what's open).

I want a logged-in area — the customer logs in with an account of their own — where they access their own documents on their own, see the history of what they've done with me, and download a duplicate copy when they need it, without asking me. The most important part: each customer can only see THEIR data; under no circumstance can one customer see another customer's document or history. Since it's people's data (name, ID document, amounts), everything has to be kept protected, and I want to be able to see the log of who accessed what.

On my side, I want a screen to associate each document with the right customer and to grant or revoke a customer's access. And I want to be able to undo it if I associate a document with the wrong person.

Once it's running, I'll want to notify the customer by WhatsApp when a new document becomes available for them in the area, and let them update their own record there.

UC-032.dor_hook: I send a satisfaction survey, see the low s, and do nothing in time — the unhappy customer just disappears.
UC-032.problema: I do send a satisfaction survey now and then, but it dies right there: I see that someone gave a low s and, in the rush, I do nothing in time. Then the unhappy customer simply disappears, and I only find out I lost them once they're already gone. The problem isn't measuring — it's acting when the s is bad, while there's still time to turn it around. I'd want something that sends the survey on its own, and that when the response is bad, right away, puts together the case with what the customer said and calls me in to recover them, before I lose them — without depending on me watching a spreadsheet of responses.
UC-032.pedido:
I provide a service to about 150 active customers and I'd like to measure their satisfaction in an organized way and, above all, act when someone is unhappy before I lose the customer. I'll start with my customer list (name, e-mail, when the last service was).

I want the system to send a short satisfaction survey by e-mail (that 0-to-10 s and a space to comment) after each service, and organize the responses by separating promoters, passives and detractors. The main point: when someone responds as a detractor (a low s), I want a recovery process to start right away — the system puts together the case with the s, the comment and that customer's history, and hands it to me to decide how to recover them. No automatic reply without me seeing it: I want to approve what's going to be done. If I don't act within a few days, the system reminds me, because a detractor left alone is a lost customer.

On my side, I want a dashboard with the overall s, the trend over time, and the list of open and closed recovery cases, so I know how many I managed to turn around.

Once it's running, I'll want to fire the survey by WhatsApp too and create different responses depending on the reason for the dissatisfaction.

Customer PII (e-mail) masked before the LLM; trail of the recovery actions; nothing fired without an OK

UC-032.kpi: % of detractors recovered (cases turned around over cases opened) and the time to first action on a detractor. In the end, you'll say: "I stopped finding out too late: a bad s becomes a case right away, with the history ready, and I win back customers who used to disappear".

UC-033.dor_hook: I send order confirmations and payment-received notices by hand, one by one, and when I forget the customer calls worried.
UC-033.problema: Every important notice to my customer goes out by hand: order confirmed, payment that landed, appointment reminder — I (or my team) type each one, and when the rush hits I forget to send it. Then the customer is left in the dark, calls worried asking whether it went through, and sometimes WhatsApp doesn't even deliver and I don't even find out. I'd want these notices to go out on their own when the event happens, through the channel the customer uses, and if it doesn't get through on one channel to try another — and I want to be able to see what was sent and what was delivered, to be sure the information got there.
UC-033.pedido:
I run a busy operation (a few hundred events a week: orders confirmed, payments received, appointments booked), and today I notify my customers of that by hand, one by one, which makes me forget notices and leaves the customer in the dark. I'll start by defining the notices I need to send (for example: "order confirmed", "payment received", "reminder for tomorrow") and, for each one, a message template with the data that goes in (customer name, order number, amount).

I want that, when the event happens, the notice goes out on its own to the customer through their preferred channel (WhatsApp, SMS or e-mail), without me typing anything. If delivery fails on one channel, the system tries another automatically, so the notice doesn't get lost. And I want to respect whoever asked not to receive them (whoever opted out no longer gets them).

On my side, I want a record of everything that was fired: which notice, to whom, over which channel, sent and delivered (or failed), so I have proof the information got there.

Once it's running, I'll want to choose the channel by notice type (collections over WhatsApp, reminders over SMS) and set the hours when notices can or can't go out.

UC-034.dor_hook: A lead that comes in through the site turns into a loose note and goes cold before anyone talks to them.
UC-034.problema: Interested people come in through my site and my campaigns, but it turns into a mess: one form sends an e-mail, another lands in a spreadsheet, and I (or my rep) only look when there's time to spare. By the time I go to respond, the lead has already gone cold — I missed the timing, and sometimes I don't even know where they came from or what they wanted. I'm paying to attract people and losing them along the way for lack of organization and speed. I'd want the lead that comes in through the page to land straight in my funnel, already organized and with a first read ready (what they want, whether it makes sense for me), and for someone to make the first contact quickly — before it goes cold — leaving me the decisions that need a person.
UC-034.pedido:
I do marketing and attract interested people through my site and through campaigns, but today that lead comes in scattered (a form that sends an e-mail, a spreadsheet) and goes cold before I respond, and a lot of sales get lost to slowness and disorganization. I'll start by defining what I need to know about a lead at capture (name, contact, what they're looking for, size/segment if it makes sense) and what my sales funnel looks like (for example: new, qualified, in contact, proposal, won/lost).

I want a public capture page I can use on my site and in campaigns, where the interested person signs up on their own, and that lead lands STRAIGHT in my funnel, without retyping, already recording where they came from. As soon as it comes in, I want an agent to do the first read — organizes the data, assesses whether the lead fits my customer profile, suggests the next step and already prepares/fires a quick welcome first contact — but the decisions that need a person (send a proposal, give a discount, drop a strategic lead) come to me to approve; nothing critical goes out without my OK.

On my side, I want to see the funnel with the leads by stage, where each one came from, and what the agent has already done and what's waiting on my decision. Since I'm collecting people's contact info on an open page, the data has to be kept protected.

Once it's running, I'll want to distribute the leads among reps automatically and prioritize whoever has the best chance to close.

UC-035.dor_hook: The front desk lives on the phone confirming and rescheduling, the calendar has no-show gaps, and patient recalls slip through the cracks.
UC-035.problema: My clinic is stuck on the phone all day: the front desk spends the day confirming appointments, rescheduling, reminding patients — and we still get no-shows, which are empty slots I never recover and money I lose. Patient recalls (the classic "come back in 6 months") almost always get forgotten, so I stop seeing people who are already my patients. Billing for anything an insurance plan doesn't cover also slips away. And it all lives in different places: the calendar in a notebook or some system, the front desk's WhatsApp, the spreadsheet of who owes. I wanted my clinic's operation organized in one place, with confirmations and reminders going out on their own, recalls triggered at the right time, and billing kept on track — with the care that patient data demands.
UC-035.pedido:
I run clinics (packed calendars, multiple providers) and my biggest pain is the front desk living on the phone confirming and rescheduling appointments, the calendar having no-show gaps, and patient recalls ("come back in 6 months") getting forgotten — plus the out-of-pocket billing that slips away. I'll start by registering the providers, the services/procedures (with duration), each provider's calendar, and the patients (name, contact, and the reason/procedure for the visit).

I want the system to confirm each appointment with the patient over WhatsApp the day before and offer to reschedule if they can't make it, to cut no-shows and immediately fill the slot that would open up. I also want a recall cadence: when it's time for a patient to come back (say, 6 months later), the system reminds them on its own to rebook. And I want to keep the out-of-pocket billing on track, sending the due-date notice and showing me who's outstanding — but nothing sensitive goes out without me being able to review it, and delicate cases come to me. Since I handle patient data and the reason for each visit, everything has to be protected and access-logged.

On my side, I want to see the calendar by provider, who confirmed, the no-shows, the pending recalls, and what's outstanding on billing.

Once it's running, I'll want a public page where the patient books a slot on their own, and a portal where they can see their recalls and receipts.

My pain is twofold: residents' requests come in loose (WhatsApp, phone, e-mail) and get lost, and dues delinquency drops off my radar until it becomes a problem. And the resident has nowhere to keep track of anything — a copy of the payment slip, the status of their request, the announcements — so they call me for everything, and my team spends the day putting out fires on the phone.
UC-045.pedido:
I run an HOA management company (about 12 buildings, each with dozens of units). My pain is twofold: residents' requests come in loose (WhatsApp, phone, e-mail) and get lost, and dues delinquency drops off my radar until it becomes a problem. And the resident has nowhere to keep track of anything — a copy of the payment slip, the status of their request, the announcements — so they call me for everything.

I'll start by registering the buildings, the units and residents, the requests, and the dues/payment slips (with payment status). I want to organize requests in a queue (open, in progress, resolved), track delinquency with a collections cadence that shows me before it sends, and have a portal where each resident logs in and sees only their own things: the requests they opened, a copy of their payment slip, the building's announcements. I want to send an announcement to the whole building at once over WhatsApp.

Nothing critical (a fine, a delinquency settlement) happens without me deciding. Each resident can only see their own unit's data, never their neighbors'. On my side, I want to see requests by building, delinquency, and the announcements sent.

Once it's running, I'll want each building's manager to have a view of just their building.

In the end, you'll say: "requests stop getting lost, delinquency stops catching me off guard, and the resident sorts things out on their own in the portal".

UC-046.dor_hook: The member stopped coming two weeks ago and you only notice when they cancel the plan.
UC-046.problema: I run a gym/studio (about 300 active members). My pain is silent attrition: the member comes less and less, disappears, and I only notice when they ask to cancel — by then it's too late. Along with that, plan renewals come due and I don't chase them in time, and members' questions (schedule, plan, monthly fee) tie up my front desk on WhatsApp all day. I lose members I could have held onto if I'd acted in time.
UC-046.pedido:
I run a gym/studio (about 300 active members). My pain is silent attrition: the member comes less and less, disappears, and I only notice when they ask to cancel — by then it's too late. I want the system to track attendance and alert me when a member is dropping off — stopped coming, pace fell — so I can try to win them back before losing them, with a message in the right tone that I approve before sending. Delicate cases — a cancellation request, a complaint, a discount — come to me to decide. The members' data stays protected. On my side, I want to see who's dropping off, the month's renewals, and the base's attendance.

Once it's running, I'll want to pull attendance from the turnstile automatically, without marking it by hand.

Panel of who's dropping off; the month's renewals; the base's attendance; member sheet

UC-046.kpi: lower churn (members won back before canceling) + on-time renewals (plan renewed before it lapses). In the end, you'll say: "I stop losing members on the quiet: when one starts dropping off, I act before they cancel".

UC-047.dor_hook: The customer calls constantly to check on the repair, and the estimate sits waiting for a "go ahead."
UC-047.problema: I run a chain of repair shops/service centers (auto or appliance). My pain is the middle of the job: the vehicle/device comes in, I do the diagnosis, I put together the estimate — and then it stalls waiting for the customer to approve, and they disappear, don't reply on WhatsApp, so the part isn't ordered and the job doesn't move. On the other side, the customer calls constantly asking "so, is it ready?", and my team stops to answer. I lose time to stalled jobs and the team lives on the phone.
UC-047.pedido:

UC-048.dor_hook: Calling employees in for exams, tracking who's done them and when each occupational-health certificate expires — all in a spreadsheet, with a legal deadline ticking.
UC-048.problema: I run an occupational health & safety (OHS) consultancy that serves several client companies. My operation is controlling the occupational-health exams (the fitness-for-work certificate) of those companies' employees: calling in whoever needs an exam (pre-hire, periodic, exit), scheduling it, making sure they showed up, receiving the report, and — most critical — controlling the expiration of each certificate, because it's a legal deadline and the client company can be fined if it lapses. Today this lives in a spreadsheet, and a certificate that expires in silence turns into a fine and a risk for my client.
UC-048.pedido:
I run an occupational health & safety (OHS) consultancy that serves several client companies. Since it's individuals' health data, everything has to be taken seriously: protected, with access logged, and I need to be able to prove later who accessed what. Nothing critical (marking a certificate complete, waiving an exam) happens without confirmation. On my side, I want to see, by company, who's up to date, who's expiring, and who's pending.

Once it's running, I'll want to send the occupational-health events to the government reporting system directly.

UC-049.dor_hook: I do the finance for dozens of companies a different way for each one, and at month-end it turns into a race against error.
UC-049.problema: I handle the finance for dozens of companies — each with its own accounts payable, receivable, and month-end close. The problem is that each client became a different way of doing things: a spreadsheet here, some control there, processes that only exist in the head of whoever does them. When someone on the team goes on vacation or a new client comes in, the operation stalls. And since it's other people's money, one wrong entry or a payment past its deadline at one company contaminates the whole month — and it's my name on the line. I wanted a **standard** operation, the same for every client, that I'd set up once and replicate for each new client, with each one isolated from the others, and with the day-to-day (collecting, paying on time, closing the month) running tracked, with the delicate stuff coming to me to decide.
UC-049.pedido:
I run a finance BPO: I handle the finances for dozens of client companies. For each one I do the same thing — I organize accounts payable and receivable, track due dates, collect from those who owe, and close the month. My problem is that today each client is a different way of doing things, all in spreadsheets and in the team's heads, so I can't standardize or grow without it becoming a mess.

I want to set up a STANDARD finance operation — the same for every client — and be able to install that same operation for each new client without rebuilding from scratch. I'll start by registering, per client: the accounts payable (vendor, amount, due date), the accounts receivable (end client, amount, due date), and the accounting period (the reference month). Each client company has to stay fully isolated from the others — one's data never shows up for another.

Day to day, I want the system to track each client's due dates on its own: warn what's coming due, what's late, and drive the collection of those who owe over WhatsApp and e-mail, continuing to remind until it's paid. At month-end, I want a close script per client that shows me what's missing. I need to see in a single dashboard, per client and consolidated, how much is payable, receivable, and at risk. No money goes out and no message is sent without me seeing it first; delicate cases (high amount, strategic client, discrepancy) come to me with the history ready. Since it's money and it's the companies' tax IDs, I want the sensitive data protected and every action logged, in a way I can undo.

Once it's running, I'll want to install this standard operation for a new client in minutes and have the system classify incoming entries on its own.

dentro: Accounts payable + receivable + accounting period (month) per client; status standardized status; spreadsheet import
Replicable standard operation: set up once, install for each new client
Durable cycle per client: tracks due dates, drives collection, monthly close script
Multi-client dashboard (per client + consolidated); isolation between clients; approval; trail + undo
evol: Reconciliation with bank/ERP via system connectors (v3)
The app organizes the finance operation, it doesn't replace the accounting system

**Multi-client dashboard with aggregation**: payable/receivable/at-risk per client and consolidated; close view by period

Agent drives the collection, summarizes what's missing at close, and escalates the delicate (high amount, discrepancy); (future) classifies entries
WhatsApp + e-mail (connected channel) for collection; bank/ERP is left to system connectors (v3)
Third-party money + **isolation between client companies** in the book; tax IDs/amounts protected; nothing goes out without an OK; trail + undo

UC-049.kpi: clients per operator without error — how many companies one analyst handles once the operation became standard, with zero payments past deadline and zero uncaught entry errors. In the end, you'll say: "I standardized the operation, I install a new client in minutes, and my team handles twice the companies without quality dropping".

objecoes.q: Can one client's data show up for another in my book?
objecoes.a: No. Each client company stays isolated from the others; one's data never shows up for another, and it's logged who accessed what.

objecoes.q: Does the system pay or clear entries on its own?
objecoes.a: No. It tracks the due dates and prepares the collection and the close, but no money goes out without your OK; clearing is yours in this version.

objecoes.q: Do I have to rebuild everything for each new client that comes in?
objecoes.a: No. You set up the standard operation once and install it for each new client; automatic bank reconciliation is a future step.

UC-050.dor_hook: I'm the receptionist for several clinics at once, and every one of their WhatsApps rings on me all day.
UC-050.problema: I run the front desk for several clinics remotely: the patient messages to book, reschedule, confirm, or ask a question, and it all lands in each clinic's WhatsApp, which rings on me all day. On my own (or with a small team) I can't keep up with the volume of all of them at once, and after hours the patient goes unanswered — by morning the queue has piled up. Each clinic has its own calendar, its own hours, its own providers, and I can't mix one with another under any circumstances. Reschedules get lost in the middle of the chats and no one prevents the no-show. I wanted each clinic's own WhatsApp to handle the basics — booking, rescheduling, confirming — on its own, with each clinic kept separate from the others, and only the delicate case reaching me.
UC-050.pedido:
I'm a remote receptionist and I run the front desk for several clinics at once. For each clinic, the patient reaches me over WhatsApp all day to book a slot, reschedule, confirm attendance, or ask a simple question. Today I answer everything by hand, the combined volume of the various clinics doesn't fit my time, and after hours the patient goes unanswered.

I want to set up a STANDARD reception operation and be able to install that same operation at each new clinic I take on, without rebuilding from scratch. Each clinic has its own calendar, its own providers, and its own hours, and one clinic can NEVER see another's patient or calendar.

For each clinic, I want the WhatsApp support to resolve things on its own, in the conversation, without the patient having to install an app or log in: when someone writes "I'd like to book," it checks availability, offers slots, and confirms; the same for rescheduling and canceling. Before appointments, I want it to send the attendance confirmation on its own and update the calendar according to the reply. On my side, I want a dashboard that shows me, per clinic, the day's calendar and the queue of conversations that need me. When the patient complains, has an urgent issue, or asks for something out of the ordinary, the support doesn't make things up: it hands it to me with the history. Since it's patient data, each clinic has to stay isolated and the personal data protected.

Once it's running, I'll want to install this reception at a new clinic in minutes and add a day-before reminder and a waitlist that fills slots that open up from cancellations.

objecoes.q: Can one clinic's calendar get mixed up with another's?
objecoes.a: No. Each clinic stays fully isolated; one never sees another's patient or calendar, and every interaction is logged.

objecoes.q: Does the patient have to download an app or create a login?
objecoes.a: No. They handle everything in the same WhatsApp conversation as always — book, reschedule, confirm — without installing anything.

objecoes.a: No. It only resolves what's within the clinic's rules; an urgent issue, a complaint, or an out-of-the-ordinary request comes to you with the history.

UC-051.dor_hook: I handle support for several companies and the volume outgrows my team — but a bot on its own loses the customer at the hard moment.
UC-051.problema: I run customer support for several companies — each one sends its own volume of questions, requests, and complaints over WhatsApp, all day long. Most of it is repetition: the same questions, the same status checks, the same simple requests. That volume doesn't fit my team unless I keep hiring nonstop, and even then, after hours the customer is left waiting. But putting a pure bot on it doesn't work either: at the hard moment — the upset customer, the off-script case, the call that needs a person — a bot on its own loses the customer. Each company has its own rules and can't be mixed with another's. I wanted support to handle the repetitive volume on its own, with quality, and pull in someone from my team only when the case truly calls for it — with the full history ready, so the customer doesn't have to repeat everything.
UC-051.pedido:
I run a customer-support BPO: I handle support for several companies at once. Each company sends its volume over WhatsApp — questions, status checks, simple requests — and most of it is repetition. My problem is that this volume doesn't fit my team without hiring nonstop, but a pure bot doesn't work either: at the hard moment the customer needs a person, and can't be left stranded with the bot.

I want support to resolve each company's repetitive volume on its own, with quality, and pull in a person only for the exception. For each client company, I'll enter the most common questions and requests and how to answer them, plus each company's rules. Each company has to stay isolated — an agent for one never sees another's customers.

Day to day, I want the agent to answer and resolve the common stuff (a question, a status, a simple request) right in the conversation, with no person touching it. When it's a sensitive case — an upset customer, a complaint, an off-script request, something that needs a decision — it doesn't make things up: it hands off to someone on my team with the full history, so the customer doesn't repeat themselves. I want a dashboard that shows me, per company, the volume that came in, how much the agent resolved on its own, and what's queued with a person, with the deadlines in plain sight. No sensitive reply goes out unchecked, and every interaction is logged. Since this is each company's end-customer data, I want it all protected and isolated by company.

Once it's running, I'll want the agent to learn from the cases the team resolved and start handling more types of case on its own.

Multi-company dashboard: volume, % auto-resolved, human queue per company; **isolation between companies**
End-customer PII protected; audit trail for every interaction
fora: Integration with each company's system (orders, ERP)
evol: Lookup into the company's system via system connectors (v3)
Agent learns from resolved cases and widens coverage (v2)

dims.t: Support/Ticket + End-customer + Client-company linked; answer and rule base per company; status standardized status
**Multi-company dashboard**: volume in × % auto-resolved × human queue, per company, with deadlines in plain sight

UC-051.kpi: % of volume the agent resolves with no human touch (per company) and cost per interaction, while keeping satisfaction steady. In the end, you'll say: "the agent resolves most of it on its own, my team only steps in where it matters, and I serve more companies without inflating headcount".

objecoes.q: Will the bot leave my customer stranded at the hard moment?
objecoes.a: No. It resolves the common cases and, on the sensitive one — an upset customer, an exception, a decision — pulls in someone from your team with the full history.

objecoes.q: Can one company's customers see another's?
objecoes.a: No. Each company stays isolated; an agent never sees another company's customers, and every interaction is logged.

objecoes.q: Does it answer anything it wants on its own?
objecoes.a: No. It only resolves what falls within the rules you set; everything else comes to a person to decide.

UC-052.dor_hook: My consultancy became a repeatable recipe, but for every new client I still build everything from scratch.
UC-052.problema: My consultancy (or my chain of locations) figured out the right way to operate — there's a method, a sequence of steps, the roles, the controls. The trouble is that this way only lives in people's heads and in slides: every time a new client comes in (or a new location opens), I build it all from scratch again, and each one comes out a little different. That holds me back: I can't grow without losing the standard, and the quality depends on whoever's executing. I wanted to turn my standard operation into something I install ready-made at each new client/location — identical, my way — where each one runs isolated, but they all follow the same method, without me rebuilding anything.
UC-052.pedido:
I run a consultancy (or a chain of locations) and I already have a right way to operate: a method with defined steps, clear roles, and controls that work. The problem is that this method only lives in the team's heads and in slide decks — for every new client or location I build from scratch, and each one comes out a little different, so I can't grow without losing the standard.

I want to turn my standard operation into a template I build once and install ready-made at each new client or location — identical, my way. I'll describe the method: the operation's steps, what each role does, the controls, and the documents I use. Each client/location has to run isolated from the others, but they all follow exactly the same template.

When a new client or location comes in, I want to install this standard operation in minutes, already configured, and start operating — without rebuilding anything. If I improve the method, I want to be able to push that improvement to the operations already installed without breaking what's running or losing data. On my side, I want to see every installed operation in one dashboard, see which ones are following the standard and where there's drift. And since each client/location has its own data, everything has to stay isolated and protected, with a record of what was done.

Once it's running, I'll want to publish this standard operation so others can install it — for it to become a product, not just my internal way of working.

objecoes.q: Will I have to build everything again for each client or location?
objecoes.a: No. You build the standard operation once and install it ready-made at each new client/location — they all run the same way.

objecoes.q: If I improve my method, do the ones already running improve too?
objecoes.a: Propagating that improvement to the operations already installed is a capability for a later phase; today the focus is standardizing and installing.

objecoes.q: Can one client's or location's data leak to another?
objecoes.a: No. Each installed operation stays isolated from the others and the data stays protected, with a record of what was done.

UC-053.dor_hook: I only find out my client's numbers slipped when they call me worried — always too late.
UC-053.problema: I'm a consultant and I track the results of several clients. The problem is that I only find out one of their numbers has slipped when they call me worried — and by then it's too late. To know how each one is doing, I keep asking for reports, spreadsheets, screenshots, and every client sends them their own way and whenever they get around to it; by the time I pull it all together, the data has already gone stale. I have no way to see, from a distance and at the right time, the metric that matters for each client — without breaking into their system and without them having to stop and send me things. I wanted a dashboard that showed me, with each client's authorization, only each one's agreed-upon metric, kept current, and that alerted me when something drifted off track — so I could act before the client feels it.
UC-053.pedido:
I'm a consultant and I track the results of several clients. Today I only find out a client's metric has slipped when they come to me worried, because to know how each one is doing I keep asking for reports and spreadsheets, and by the time I pull it all together the data is already old.

I want a dashboard that shows me, in one place, the metric that matters for each client — kept current — but with one non-negotiable rule: each client explicitly authorizes what I'm allowed to see of their business. Nothing shows up for me without the client's consent, and they can view and revoke that authorization at any time. I see only the agreed-upon slice, never the client's whole system.

With the clients who authorized me, I want to track each metric over time, compare clients, and get an alert when one drifts off track — so I can act before the client feels it. Every access to a client's data has to be logged, in a way I can audit later to see who saw what and when. One client must never see another's data.

Once it's running, I'll want the system to suggest the next action for the client whose metric has drifted off track, drawing on the history of what has worked before.

Metrics dashboard per client (only those who consented) + trend over time + comparison
Alert when a metric drifts off track; auditable trail of every access

Data from a client who didn't consent
evol: Never — the model is a consented slice, not full access
Suggested next action for the consultant (v2)

objecoes.q: Will the consultant see everything about my business?
objecoes.a: No. You explicitly authorize only the slice they can see, and you can revoke it whenever you want; they never access your whole system.

objecoes.q: Can one client see another's data in the consultant's dashboard?
objecoes.a: No. Each client stays isolated; every access is logged in a way you can audit to see who saw what and when.

objecoes.q: And what if I don't want to share anything?
objecoes.a: Then nothing of yours shows up — without your consent, the consultant doesn't see your metric.

UC-054.dor_hook: I manage a portfolio of companies, but I can only see each one separately — never the whole on a single screen.
UC-054.problema: I manage a portfolio of several companies — each has its own system, its own results, its own way of reporting. My problem is that I can only look at one at a time: to get the full picture, I pull spreadsheet after spreadsheet by hand, and by the time I'm done the numbers have already changed. I have no way to see the whole portfolio consolidated — how the results add up, which companies drag it down, where the risk sits — without depending on each one to send me the data whenever they get around to it. And I can't just walk into each company's system: each one owns what it shares. I wanted a screen that showed the aggregated portfolio, with each company's authorization over what counts toward it, kept current, so I could decide by looking at the whole — no spreadsheet hunt and without breaking into anyone's system.
UC-054.pedido:
I manage a portfolio of several companies and today I can only look at one at a time. To get the picture of the whole, I pull spreadsheet after spreadsheet by hand, and by the time I'm done the numbers have already changed. Nothing counts without the company's consent, and they can view and revoke that authorization whenever they want. I see the consolidated total and the slice each company released, never their internal system.

With the companies that authorized it, I want to track the consolidated total over time, drill down by company, and get an alert when the whole (or one company within it) drifts off track. Every access to a company's data has to be logged, auditable — who saw what and when. One company in the portfolio must never see another's data.

Once it's running, I'll want to compare my portfolio against a market benchmark, aggregated and anonymous, without exposing any single company.

In the end, you'll say: "I see my whole portfolio on one screen, with each company's authorization, and I decide by looking at the whole — without piecing spreadsheets together by hand".

objecoes.a: No. Your company authorizes only the slice that counts toward the consolidated view, and can revoke it whenever you want; the internal system is never accessed.

objecoes.q: Can one company in the portfolio see another's data?
objecoes.a: No. Each company stays isolated; the consolidated view doesn't let you re-identify a company without its authorization, and every access is logged and auditable.

objecoes.q: And what if a company doesn't want to be part of the consolidated view?
objecoes.a: Then it doesn't count — without the company's consent, none of its data is aggregated.

UC-055.dor_hook: I wanted to start with a ready-made operation and just tune it to my business, without building everything from scratch.
UC-055.problema: I'm just starting out (or I want to replace my current setup) and I don't want to build my system from scratch: picking objects, designing screens, configuring everything. Every time I try, I get stuck on the blank page — I don't even know where to begin. I wanted to take a ready-made operation, built by someone who knows the field, with the essentials already working, and just tune it to my business: my name, my data, my way. And as I grow, to be able to evolve this app without losing what's already running. What I don't want is to start from scratch or depend on an expensive, drawn-out project just to get the basics standing.
UC-055.pedido:
I want to start my operation without building everything from scratch. I saw a ready-made accelerator — an operation already built, with the essentials working — and I want to install that base in my business and just tune it to my way.

I'll want to start from the ready-made operation and adapt it: put in my business's name and identity, import my initial data (from my spreadsheet, without retyping), and change what doesn't fit — swap a field, a step, a piece of copy — without breaking what comes ready-made. I need to understand what the accelerator does before I install it, and start operating in minutes, not weeks.

Once installed, I want it to be MINE: the data is mine and stays isolated, and as my business changes I can evolve the app by talking to it, without losing what's already running. If a better version of the accelerator comes out, I want to be able to update without losing my data or my customizations.

Once it's running, I'll want to turn on more parts of the operation as I need them, at my own pace.

dentro: Ready-made accelerator (app-snapshot + adapter + agent), installable; understand before installing
Install + adapt (identity, data, tweaks) without breaking the ready-made part; data isolated per instance
Distribution via **track A** (self-serve template) already in Phase 1; evolution by conversation while preserving data
Accelerator version updates preserving data and customizations
fora: creator payout/creator payout (this is first-party, no take-rate)
Store open to third parties in v1
evol: Accelerators from external creators with creator payout (UC-056/057/058)

dims.t: The accelerator as an installable package (app snapshot + adapter + agent) + the installed instance with its own data
The accelerator's listing (understand before installing) + install/adapt flow + the screens of the ready-made operation
Install → adapt → operate; accelerator version updates preserving data; evolution by conversation

In the end, you'll say: "I installed a ready-made operation and within minutes it was running with my own look, no expensive project and no starting from scratch".

objecoes.q: Will I be stuck with the accelerator exactly as it came?
objecoes.a: No. Once installed it's yours: you adapt it to your business and evolve it by talking to the app, without losing what's already running.

objecoes.q: If a better version comes out, do I lose my data or my changes?
objecoes.a: That's not the intent: an update preserves your data and your customizations; the full form of this in the Store is a capability for a later phase.

objecoes.q: Do I need to know how to build a system to use it?
objecoes.a: No. You install a ready-made operation and tune it to your way; you start operating in minutes, with no from-scratch project.

UC-056.dor_hook: I know my niche inside out and I serve clients one by one by hand, but I can't turn that expertise into something that sells on its own.
UC-056.problema: I live and breathe my niche — I know exactly how the operation of people who do what I do should work, because it's my day-to-day. I've built (or want to build) the perfect system for my kind of business, the way generic tools never get right. But that knowledge stays trapped in me: I serve clients one by one by hand, and I have no way to turn it into a product that others in my niche install and that pays me for it. The off-the-shelf systems on the market are too big or too generic; nobody speaks my niche's language. I wanted to take my vertical — the operation I know works — and publish it for others in the same field to install, adapt to their business, and use, and to get paid for each one who adopts it.
UC-056.pedido:
I've mastered a niche and I know how the operation of the people who work in it should run. I want to take the vertical I built — the right operation for my kind of business, with the objects, the screens, and the flow that generic systems never get right — and turn it into a product that others in my niche install and use.

I'll start from my already-built operation and prepare it to become an installable product: define what comes ready out of the box, what each client will be able to adapt (the name, the fields, the copy), and what they shouldn't touch so it doesn't break. I want a listing that explains, to someone in my niche, what the vertical does and why it fits better than the generic option.

When someone in my niche installs it, they have to be up and operating fast, adapt it to their business without breaking what comes ready-made, and their data stays isolated from mine and from everyone else's. I want to be paid for each client who adopts it and to track how many installed it and how they're using it. If I improve the vertical, I want to be able to publish the new version without breaking anyone already running it or making anyone lose data.

objecoes.a: The goal is to publish the new version without breaking anyone already running it or making anyone lose data; the full form of this comes with the Store.

UC-057.dor_hook: My methodology works, but it stays trapped in PDFs and hourly consulting — it never became a product that scales.
UC-057.problema: I have a methodology that works — a structured way to solve my clients' problem, with steps, tools, and deliverables that get results. The problem is that it only comes to life when I (or someone on my team) sits with the client hour by hour: it stays trapped in PDFs, spreadsheets, and slide decks. It doesn't scale — my ceiling is the number of hours I can sell, and when I stop showing up, the method stops for the client. I've tried turning it into a course, but a course teaches and then it's gone; nobody operates it. I wanted to turn my methodology into a product that the client installs and that walks them through the steps of the method on its own, applying what I know without me being in the room — and to get paid for each client who adopts it, not by the hour.
UC-057.pedido:
I have my own methodology that gets results: a structured way to solve my clients' problem, with steps, tools, and deliverables. Today it only happens when I sit with the client hour by hour — it lives in PDFs and slide decks, doesn't scale, and when I step off the stage the method stops.

I want to turn my methodology into an installable product that walks the client through the steps of the method on its own, applying what I know without me being in the room. I'll describe the methodology: the stages in the right order, what the client does in each one, the controls, the deliverables, and how I know they've moved forward. I want to define what comes ready-made and what each client adapts to their own context.

When a client installs it, the product has to walk them through the methodology — show the current step, chase down what's missing, generate each stage's deliverable — and each client stays isolated from the others, with their data protected. I want to be paid for each client who adopts it and to see how many are at each stage of the method. If I evolve the methodology, I want to publish the new version without breaking anyone who's in the middle of it.

I can see that my standard operation is, in practice, already a product — it's just tied to labor. I wanted to package that operation and sell it as a system that others install and use (with or without my team operating behind the scenes), so I stop depending only on selling my time and start getting paid for each client who adopts the package.

UC-059.dor_hook: My policies live in a Word doc and the sign-offs in some lost e-mail — if an audit hits, I have no way to prove anything.
UC-059.problema: I have to keep my company current with the LGPD and my industry's rules, but today it all lives scattered: the privacy policy is in a Word doc nobody reads, the employees' sign-offs are in lost e-mails, and when a non-conformance comes up I handle it on the fly. If an audit or a regulator comes knocking, I have no way to show, in an organized way, that the policies exist, that they were accepted, who accepted them and when, and what I did when something went off-standard. Every company in my industry struggles with exactly the same thing — and each time starts from scratch.
UC-059.pedido:
I run the compliance/privacy function at a services company and I need to organize everything in one place, in a way I can prove later. To start, I have a list of the policies that exist (name, version, date, the department it applies to), the list of employees who need to sign off (name, area, e-mail), and a loose history of incidents I've already handled.

I want a system that keeps each policy with its version, sends it to the right employees to sign off, and tracks who has signed off and who's still pending — chasing down the stragglers on its own over the weeks. When a non-conformance comes up (a deviation, a complaint, an incident), I want to log the case, classify its severity, assign an owner and a deadline, and follow it through to closure — nothing critical should be closed without my approval.

Since this is audit material, I want every important thing — the policy that was in force, who signed off, when, who handled the non-conformance and how — logged in a way I can present later and that no one can tamper with. The employees' data is sensitive and needs to stay protected.

Once it's running, I'll want to be able to package this operation as a ready-made template that other companies in my industry can adopt with one click, keeping the structure but with their own data.

evol: **Template distribution in the store via app store**
creator payout to the template's creator (creator payout)
